elements.name: Description
texts: The poetry of Patrick Kavanagh's 'Lough Derg', detailing a visit in 1941

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

elements.name: Text
texts:
“The bell brought the sleepers from their cubicles. 
Grey-faced boatmen were getting out a boat.
Mass was said. Another day began.
The penance wheel turned round again.
Pilgrims went out in boats, singing
‘O Fare Thee Well, Lough Derg’ as they waved
Affection to the persecuting stones.”
